request_body.variables: map of operation variables — pagination cursors travel HERE, never spliced into the document

endpoint.wrong_path_behavior: any path other than the versioned graphql endpoint answers 404 with the standard REST error array (errorCode NOT_FOUND); only the graphql endpoint is supported

operation_document.shape: query ($first: Int, $after: String) { uiapi { query { Account(first: $first, after: $after) { edges { node { Id Name { value } Industry { value } AnnualRevenue { value } Website { value } } cursor } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } totalCount } } } }
rules: Id is a plain scalar; every other field is a field-value object read through its value field
the document must be identical on every page of a scan; only variables.first / variables.after change
cursor strings are opaque and must never appear inside the document text

pagination.rule: while hasNextPage, pass the page's endCursor as the next request's after variable; first page sends after null or omits it
pagination.totals: totalCount reports the full result-set size on every page

graphql_errors.status: HTTP 200 even when the request contains errors

entry.path: optional list locating the failing field

extensions.classification: ErrorType: InvalidSyntax | ValidationError | DataFetchingException | OperationNotSupported | ExecutionAborted

graphql_errors.partial_data_rule: errors can accompany partial data; records that did decode must be kept and the errors surfaced as warnings
graphql_errors.total_failure_rule: data null (or missing) with errors present is a query failure and must raise

field_level_security.schema_rule: the schema honors the context user's object- and field-level security; a field without FLS read access is absent from the schema and querying it is a ValidationError
field_level_security.null_rule: an accessible field with no data returns {"value": null}; null means empty, not hidden — decode it as a null field value, never drop the record

client_rules: the access token never appears in exception text
records decode in edge order across pages; a full scan returns totalCount records exactly once each
